results.title: [PDF] SpatialClaw: Rethinking Action Interface for Agentic Spatial Reasoning
[Literature Review] SpatialClaw: Rethinking Action Interface for Agentic Spatial Reasoning
results.content:
Contact 02-6925-6978 E-mail: moonlight@corca.ai

┬® 2026 Corca, Inc. All rights reserved. [...] The `tools.Geometry` class, for instance, provides primitives like `euclidean_distance(p1, p2)` for 3D points, `angle_between_vectors(v1, v2)` for degree angles, and `project_point_to_camera(point_3d, c2w, fx, fy, cx, cy)` for projecting 3D world points to pixel coordinates. The `tools.Mask` class offers mask-specific operations like `centroid(mask)`, `area(mask)`, `bounding_box(mask)`, and `iou(mask_a, mask_b)`.

`tools.Geometry`
`euclidean_distance(p1, p2)`
`angle_between_vectors(v1, v2)`
`project_point_to_camera(point_3d, c2w, fx, fy, cx, cy)`
`tools.Mask`
`centroid(mask)`
`area(mask)`
`bounding_box(mask)`
`iou(mask_a, mask_b)` [...] Spatial reasoning, which involves understanding object locations, relationships, and movements in 3D, remains a significant challenge for Vision-Language Models (VLMs). While tool-augmented agents enhance VLMs with specialized perception modules, their efficacy is constrained by the design of the action interface used to invoke these tools. Existing spatial agents typically employ either single-pass code execution, which necessitates committing to a complete analysis strategy before observing intermediate results, or rely on a structured tool-call interface, which often lacks the flexibility for composing operations or tailoring analyses to specific tasks. Both approaches offer limited adaptability for open-ended, complex 3D/4D spatial reasoning.
